name: Checks Cashed 1%
description_short: Checks Cashed 1% is a check-cashing service in Glendale, Arizona charging 1% plus $1 fee per check, with no limits on check amounts and MoneyGram services available.
description_long:
Checks Cashed 1%, LLC has been operating in the Glendale, Arizona area for over a decade as a check-cashing and financial services provider. The company positions itself as a low-cost alternative to traditional check-cashing services, targeting customers who need immediate access to funds without excessive fees. They operate from a single physical location at 5270 N. 59th Ave. in Glendale and can be reached by phone at 623-842-2610.

The company's primary service is check cashing with a flat fee structure of 1% of the check amount plus $1. They cash multiple types of checks including payroll checks, personal checks, government checks (tax refunds, Social Security), insurance payments, money orders, business checks, settlement receipts, inheritance payments, and lottery winnings. Additionally, they function as an authorized MoneyGram agent, offering money orders, bill payment services, and money transfers. The company claims no upper limit on check amounts, stating they can cash checks ranging from $100 to $100,000.

Checks Cashed 1% differentiates itself primarily through its transparent, competitive fee structure and stated commitment to not charging additional fees beyond their standard 1% plus $1 rate. The company explicitly markets its refusal to turn away customers or add surprise fees, positioning this as a customer-friendly approach compared to competitors. They accept government-issued ID, military IDs, and passports as valid identification.

The primary limitation of this business is its physical location constraint—customers must visit the single Glendale storefront during business hours to access services. While the company claims competitive rates, the actual competitiveness of 1% plus $1 depends on individual check amounts and local market comparison. The website provides no information about check verification processes, hold policies, or what constitutes acceptable identification beyond the general categories mentioned. No online services, remote check deposit, or alternative access methods are mentioned.
pros: Transparent, simple fee structure: flat 1% plus $1 with no hidden or variable fees
No upper limit on check amounts—claims ability to cash checks up to $100,000
Wide variety of check types accepted including government, insurance, business, inheritance, and lottery checks
Authorized MoneyGram agent providing money orders, bill payment, and money transfer services
Long operational history in Glendale (over a decade) providing stability and community presence
Flexible ID acceptance including government-issued IDs, military IDs, and passports
Same-day cash availability—company states checks can be cashed in minutes
cons: Single physical location limits accessibility to Glendale-area customers only; no online or mobile check cashing options
No information provided about check hold policies, verification procedures, or acceptance criteria beyond ID requirement
No published business hours listed on website, creating uncertainty about service availability
Fee structure (1% + $1) may not be competitive for smaller checks; for a $100 check, the fee equals 2% effective rate
No mention of additional financial products or services beyond check cashing and MoneyGram
best_for: Glendale-area residents and workers who receive paychecks or government checks and need immediate cash access
Customers cashing larger checks ($1,000+) where 1% fee becomes more competitive relative to flat-fee competitors
Individuals without access to traditional banking who need supplementary money transfer and bill payment services
diagnosis: Checks Cashed 1% is best for Glendale, Arizona residents who need fast check cashing and occasional money transfer services. The main caveat is that this is a single-location, in-person-only service with limited transparency about operational details like hours, hold policies, and exact acceptance criteria—customers must visit in person or call for specifics.
services: Government check cashing (tax refunds, Social Security)
